classification: real-driving-sequence reconstruction-based counterfactual closed-loop neural simulator
log_driven_definition: A simulator that constructs an interactive environment from real road-driving capture sequences and generates counterfactual closed-loop evolution; exact log replay is not required.

evidence_chain.name: log_reproduction
evidence: the released scene is associated with a real-driving dataset
the released scene and scenario assets run reproducibly
scene asset identity and local checksum are recorded
evidence_chain.main_gap: Exact source frames, timestamps, camera poses, and matched real-versus-reconstructed observations have not been audited.

evidence_chain.name: sensor_consistency
evidence: synchronized RGB, semantic, and depth outputs
multi-camera observations over a bounded rollout
internal cross-modal support for the inserted actor
evidence_chain.main_gap: Visible synthetic and domain artifacts remain, and no comparison with real sensor observations is available.

evidence_chain.name: task_level_consistency
accepted_subclaims: ego states and actions are strictly paired across the three conditions
the internal geometry and metric stack responds differently to actor placements at lateral 0.0 m and 3.5 m
evidence_chain.main_gap: The test covers only two exact actor placements, no actor motion sweep, and limited causal coverage.

evidence_chain.name: closed_loop_outcome_credibility
evidence: the simulator-side observation, plan, action, state-update, rollout, and scoring path executes reproducibly
evidence_chain.main_gap: The deterministic planner is a simulator-loop enabler rather than a sensor-input AD agent, so no AD-performance conclusion is supported.

current_claim: HUGSIM is a suitable first carrier for developing the four-layer credibility evidence chain. Current evidence supports a narrow internal task-level response but does not establish credible AD-system closed-loop outcomes.
